title: High Molecular Weight DNA Extraction for Marine Macroalgal Tissue
author: Malia Moore
materials: To each pellet, add 10 mL G2 buffer, from the QIAGEN kit. Incubate at 50 °C for 30-60 minutes, or until the pellet is dissolved. Swirl the pellet to mix but do not try to pipette or vortex.
Equilibrate a QIAGEN Genomic-tip 100/G column with 4 mL of Buffer QBT.
Discard the supernatant without disturbing the pellet.

steps.title: Flash-freeze algal tissue in liquid nitrogen...
steps.instruction: Flash-freeze algal tissue in liquid nitrogen (target ≥ 5 g wet tissue).

steps.title: Prepare desired volume of Carlson lysis...
steps.instruction: Prepare desired volume of Carlson lysis buffer ( 100 millimolar (mM) Tris-HCl, pH 9.5 , 2% CTAB, 1.4 Molarity (M) NaCl, 1% PEG 8000, 20 millimolar (mM) EDTA) and mix Overnight on a magnetic stirrer. The stock solutions suggested under consumables will yield a homogenous buffer with no precipitate.

steps.title: Quickly transfer sample to lyophilization container...
steps.instruction: Quickly transfer sample to lyophilization container and freeze dry for 36-48 hours.

steps.title: Macerate the tissue with a clean...
steps.instruction: Macerate the tissue with a clean spatula to increase surface area and put on the lyophilizer for another 24:00:00 .

steps.title: Remove and refrigerate with desiccant for...
steps.instruction: Remove and refrigerate with desiccant for immediate use, or store at -80 °C for longer periods.

steps.title: Pre-heat a heat block or water...
steps.instruction: Pre-heat a heat block or water bath to 65 °C and place in a fume hood.

steps.title: For each extraction, transfer 20 mL...
steps.instruction: For each extraction, transfer 20 mL of Carlson lysis buffer to a 50-ml Falcon tube.

steps.title: In a fume hood, add 400...
steps.instruction: In a fume hood, add 400 µL BME (originally 50 µL ) and mix by vortexing. Pre-warm the solution to 65 °C for 00:30:00 before starting the extraction.

steps.title: Scoop 0
steps.instruction: Scoop 0.5 teaspoons lyophilized plant tissue into a clean mortar and add 50-100 mg powdered PVPP. Grind with pestle for ~ 00:00:30 , until tissue is powdered and combined, but not long enough to introduce significant moisture. Move immediately into DNA extraction.

steps.title: Pour tissue into the warm buffer
steps.instruction: Pour tissue into the warm buffer. Invert 5 times.

steps.title: Add 40 µL of RNase A and vortex for 00:00:05
steps.instruction: Add 40 µL of RNase A and vortex for 00:00:05 .

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional : If using a heat block with mixing, set the block (still at 65 °C ) to mixing at 300 rpm, °C, 5 minutes .

steps.title: Incubate for 01:00:00 at 65 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate for 01:00:00 at 65 °C .

steps.title: Allow the tubes to cool down...
steps.instruction: Allow the tubes to cool down to Room temperature for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Add 20 mL chloroform and vortex...
steps.instruction: Add 20 mL chloroform and vortex for two pulses of 00:00:05 each.

steps.title: Centrifuge the tubes at 3500 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tubes at 3500 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes .

steps.title: In a fume hood, transfer the...
steps.instruction: In a fume hood, transfer the top layer of lysate from each tube to a new 50-ml Falcon tube, without disturbing the interphase. Tip : The lysate layer should be 14-18 mL of solution, but it is recommended to use widebore tips, transferring 1 mL at a time. Tips can also be widened by cutting standard P1000 tips.

steps.title: Mix supernatant with 0
steps.instruction: Mix supernatant with 0.4X 5 Molarity (M) potassium acetate (KOAc) at Room temperature , inverting at least 10 times to combine, then incubate On ice for 00:20:00 .

steps.title: Centrifuge the tubes at 3500 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tubes at 3500 x g, 4 °C, 45 minutes .

steps.title: Remove and retain the supernatant. Tip
steps.instruction: Remove and retain the supernatant. Tip : This may best be done by pouring slowly and observing the polysaccharide-salt pellet, which may be mobile. Leave some liquid behind in favor of avoiding the pellet.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.7X volumes of isopropanol. Invert 10 times. Incubate at -80 °C for 00:15:00 . Do not extend this incubation.

steps.title: Centrifuge the sample at 3500 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample at 3500 x g, 4 °C, 45 minutes . Tip : If available, a fixed-angle centrifuge will create a pellet on the wall of the tube that has greater surface area for dissolution in step 24 (as compared to a conical pellet at the base of a falcon tube from a swinging bucket).

steps.title: Discard the supernatant without disturbing the pellet
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant without disturbing the pellet. Use sterile wipes to absorb the liquid on the tube walls, being careful not to disturb the pellet.

steps.title: Pour the DNA in G2 buffer...
steps.instruction: Pour the DNA in G2 buffer through the equilibrated column and allow it to flow through with just gravity.

steps.title: Once all the lysate has passed...
steps.instruction: Once all the lysate has passed through, wash the column with 8 mL of Buffer QC.

steps.title: Repeat the wash with another 8 mL of Buffer QC
steps.instruction: Repeat the wash with another 8 mL of Buffer QC.

steps.title: Place the column over a clean...
steps.instruction: Place the column over a clean 50-mL Falcon tube, and elute the genomic DNA with 5 mL of Buffer QF, pre-warmed to 55 °C .

steps.title: Allow the eluate to cool down to Room temperature
steps.instruction: Allow the eluate to cool down to Room temperature .

steps.title: Add 3
steps.instruction: Add 3.5 mL of isopropanol to the eluted DNA and mix by inverting the tube 10 times.

steps.title: Incubate the tube at -20 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate the tube at -20 °C for at least 3 hours, or Overnight .

steps.title: Centrifuge the tube at 3500 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tube at 3500 x g, 4 °C, 45 minutes .

steps.title: Add 4 mL of ice-cold 70%...
steps.instruction: Add 4 mL of ice-cold 70% ethanol to the pelleted DNA and invert the tube 10 times.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 3500 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 3500 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes . Tip : If using a swinging bucket centrifuge the DNA will pellet at the base of the tube and be easy to locate and resuspend. If using a fixed angle, mark the side of the tube that faces outwards in order to locate the pellet for washes and elution.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant without disturbing the pellet
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant without disturbing the pellet. Use sterile wipes to dry the tube walls, being careful not to disturb the pellet.

steps.title: Resuspend the DNA in 100 µL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the DNA in 100 µL of TE buffer and incubate at Room temperature , typically Overnight .

steps.title: Transfer the DNA into a nuclease-free 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the DNA into a nuclease-free 1.5-mL tube (DNA LoBind tube preferred) using a wide-bore tip, and store at 4 °C . Tip : Often, waiting a further 48:00:00 before quantifying on Nanodrop and Qubit will allow the DNA to further relax and yield the most accurate results

steps.title: Carry samples forward to BluePippin size selection, if available
steps.instruction: Carry samples forward to BluePippin size selection, if available. This gel separation will retain DNA fragments greater than 15 kb and discard any residual contamination still evident on a Nanodrop trace. For these benefits, expect 50-70% loss of DNA.

steps.title: At 30 minutes, add another 40...
steps.instruction: At 30 minutes, add another 40 µL of RNase A, inverting 10 times to combine.
